tool_name: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area

inputs.name: Heating Steam Pressure (P_steam)
inputs.note: Steam saturation temp at 3 bar(a) = 133°C; 1.5 bar(a) = 112°C
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Evaporator Operating Pressure (P_evap)
inputs.note: Vacuum evaporation at 0.3 bar(a) → T_sat = 69°C; reduces thermal degradation
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Boiling Point Rise of Solution (BPR)
inputs.note: At product concentration: NaCl at 20% → BPR ≈ 2.6 K; sugar at 60% → BPR ≈ 5 K; caustic at 40% → BPR ≈ 20 K

inputs.name: Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient (U)
inputs.note: Falling film (water) ≈ 3000–5000; forced circulation (viscous) ≈ 1000–2500; natural circ. ≈ 1500–3000

formulas: V_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h * (1 - feed_conc_pct / product_conc_pct); P_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h - V_kg_h   // [kg/h] Water Evaporation Rate (V) from Mass Balance Reference: Overall mass balance: F = V + P; solids balance: F·x_F = P·x_P → V = F(1 − x_F/x_P)

T_steam_c = 100 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_evap_sat_c = 100 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_boil_c = T_evap_sat_c + BPR_k   // [°C] Steam Saturation Temperatures Reference: Antoine approximation for water saturation temperature — Perry's 9th Ed. §2; BPR elevates boiling point above T_sat
dT_eff = T_steam_c - T_boil_c   // [K] Net Temperature Driving Force (ΔTLM) Reference: Effective ΔT = T_steam − T_boil; T_boil = T_evap,sat + BPR — TEMA / Perry's §11.12

lambda_steam_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c; cp_feed_kj_kgk = 4.18 * (1 - feed_conc_pct/100) + 1.5 * feed_conc_pct/100; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_boil_c; Q_preheat_kw = feed_flow_kg_h / 3600 * cp_feed_kj_kgk * (T_boil_c - feed_temp_c); Q_evap_kw = V_kg_h / 3600 * lambda_evap_kj_kg; Q_steam_kw = Q_preheat_kw + Q_evap_kw   // [kW] Steam Heat Duty — Including Feed Pre-Heating (Q_steam) Reference: Total duty = preheat load + evaporation load; λ_steam from Antoine saturation enthalpy approximation

S_kg_h = Q_steam_kw * 3600 / (2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c); steam_economy = V_kg_h / S_kg_h   // [kg/h, kg water/kg steam] Steam Consumption & Economy Reference: Steam economy = kg evaporation / kg steam consumed; single-effect ≈ 0.8–0.9; triple-effect ≈ 2.5–2.8

A_evap_m2 = Q_steam_kw * 1000 / (U_overall_w_m2k * dT_eff)   // [m²] Required Heat Transfer Area (A_evap) Reference: A = Q / (U·ΔT) — fundamental heat transfer equation; TEMA / GEA falling film evaporator design

standards: Alfa Laval / GEA Evaporator Engineering Practice

V1.error_msg: Product concentration must exceed feed concentration — check x_F and x_P inputs

V2.error_msg: Effective ΔT < 5 K: very small driving force — heat transfer area will be enormous; increase steam pressure or reduce evaporator pressure (deeper vacuum)

V3.error_msg: Steam economy < 0.7: below single-effect minimum — heat losses may be underestimated; verify feed preheat duty and BPR value

smart_warnings.message: ΔT_eff < 10 K: economically marginal single-effect operation — evaluate multiple-effect evaporation (economy improves linearly with number of effects)
BPR > 15 K: large boiling point rise severely reduces ΔT and increases steam consumption — evaluate mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) for high-BPR solutions
Large heat transfer area (> 1000 m²): multiple evaporator bodies likely required — evaluate falling film with large-diameter tubes for reduced fouling
High boiling temperature at elevated concentration: thermal degradation risk for heat-sensitive products (food, pharma) — deepen vacuum to reduce T_boil
Low economy at large evaporation rate: high operating cost — each additional effect saves ~35–40% steam; payback on triple-effect typically < 2 years

fmea.effect: Product concentration must exceed feed concentration — check x_F and x_P inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Effective ΔT < 5 K: very small driving force — heat transfer area will be enormous; increase steam pressure or reduce evaporator pressure (deeper vacuum)

fmea.failureMode: ΔT_eff < 10 K: economically marginal single-effect operation — evaluate multiple-effect evaporation (economy improves linearly with number of effects)
BPR > 15 K: large boiling point rise severely reduces ΔT and increases steam consumption — evaluate mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) for high-BPR solutions
Large heat transfer area (> 1000 m²): multiple evaporator bodies likely required — evaluate falling film with large-diameter tubes for reduced fouling
High boiling temperature at elevated concentration: thermal degradation risk for heat-sensitive products (food, pharma) — deepen vacuum to reduce T_boil
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_boil_c > 100 && product_conc_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: Low economy at large evaporation rate: high operating cost — each additional effect saves ~35–40% steam; payback on triple-effect typically < 2 years
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: steam_economy < 1.0 && V_kg_h > 10000
